history.author: Hassam Nasir
history.titleZh: 英特尔传闻已久的酷睿Ultra 3 205处理器已在法国零售商处上市销售，定价约合182美元，早期列表信息确认了该芯片已知规格。
history.descriptionZh:
英特尔低端桌面处理器Core Ultra 3 205近期在法国零售商PC21重新现身，定价158欧元（含税），但暂无库存。这款隶属于Arrow Lake家族、核心代号为Core Ultra 200S系列的芯片早在去年就已传出消息，甚至有过非零售版的完整评测，却始终未正式发布。此次 listing 距离上次同一零售商的短暂出现已相隔七个月，进一步印证了其作为OEM专属型号、仅面向系统集成商的定位——事实上，英特尔从未为Arrow Lake桌面系列推出过任何正式发布的Core Ultra 3型号。

从技术规格来看，Core Ultra 3 205采用8核设计，包括4个性能核（P-core）与4个能效核（E-core），最高睿频可达4.9 GHz，并集成2个Xe架构核显核心。其热设计功耗仅为57W，能效表现突出。根据早期测试，其多核性能比同样定位入门级的酷睿i3-14100高出约30%，甚至超越前代酷睿i5同级产品，因此一度被业界视为新一代高性价比入门级芯片的有力竞争者。

此次零售商页面信息部分印证了此前泄露的规格，包括15MB的L2缓存以及4.9 GHz的最高睿频，所用部件编号AT8076806773也与英特尔一年前内部文档相符。该处理器在2025年12月也曾于德国零售商处以148欧元（不含税）价格短暂出现。

然而，随着英特尔已对Arrow Lake家族进行更新，推出Core Ultra 200K Plus系列，这款Core Ultra 3 205很可能已无缘正式发布。尽管其展现出优秀的能效比和市场潜力，但英特尔始终未将其列入官方零售序列，也未推出盒装版本。页面标注的“tray”（散片）进一步说明它仅以批量方式供应给系统制造商，而非面向普通消费者零售。

总体而言，Core Ultra 3 205是一款曾引发关注但最终止步于传闻的OEM特供芯片，其消失也折射出英特尔在入门级桌面市场产品策略的调整。对于追求高能效入门配置的用户而言，它的缺席无疑是一种遗憾。

history.title: Jensen Huang says Nvidia engineers should use AI tokens worth half their annual salary every year to be fully productive — compares not using AI to using paper and pencil for designing chips
history.description: The Nvidia chief said that he will be alarmed if his engineers did not use AI tokens amounting to about half their annual salary, saying that AI tools will 10x their productivity.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 9 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said that he’ll be deeply alarmed if an engineer getting paid $500,000 a year does not consume at $250,000 worth of AI tokens to get their job done. The leather-clad chief of the world’s most valuable AI company said this during an episode of the All-In Podcast shot on the last day of Nvidia GTC 2026, after one of the hosts asked Huang if he’s spending around $2 billion a year on tokens for the Nvidia engineering team, with Jensen answering, “We’re trying to.” Jensen Huang: Nvidia's Future, Physical AI, Rise of the Agent, Inference Explosion, AI PR Crisis - YouTube Watch On “Let me give you a thought experiment. Let’s say you have a software engineer or AI researcher, and you pay them $500,000 a year,” the Nvidia CEO said. He also added, “At the end of the year, I’m going to ask him how much did you spend in tokens. And [if] that person said $5,000, I will go ape something else. If that $500,000 engineer did not consume at least $250,000 worth of tokens, I am going to be deeply alarmed.” Huang went on to compare an employee not using AI tokens to a chip designer saying that they will use paper and pencil and eschew CAD tools to get their work done.Jensen compared today’s AI tools to machinery that was invented during the industrial revolution, which allowed workers to physically do things that, in the past, were either too heavy, too big, or took too long. Now, it seems that the Nvidia CEO wants to use AI for mental tasks so that Nvidia engineers can focus on creativity. “It’s just a new way of doing computer programming. In the past, we code. In the future, we’re going to write ideas, architectures, specifications,” Huang said. “I think that every engineer is going to have a hundred agents.”Article continues below You may like Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang says, "I love constraints," praising the ongoing shortages caused by AI Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang says robots could be 'AI immigrants' that can address labor shortages Jensen Huang expects Nvidia to sell $1 trillion of AI hardware through 2027 It seems that the Nvidia CEO isn’t the only one investing in AI tokens for his employees to freely use. According to Business Insider, many tech companies are now starting to offer guaranteed access to AI inference power as part of the remuneration package to candidates. That thought is that by giving people generous amounts of AI tokens, they’d be able to amplify their productivity by as much as 10 times or more.However, using AI in the corporate setting is not without its issues. There have been reports that more than half of CEOs have yet to see clear benefits from AI deployments, with only about 12% getting higher revenues and reduces costs. We’ve also seen several AI-related outages with Amazon Web Services, with one report saying that Amazon has called its engineers for a meeting to discuss issues created by “Gen-AI assisted changes” that had “high blast radius.” And although Microsoft did not say anything about AI, it promised to fix Windows 11’s most annoying flaws to restore its reputation several months after its CEO revealed that AI writes up to 30% of Microsoft’s code. history.author: Jowi Morales
history.titleZh: 英伟达CEO黄仁勋称工程师需每年使用相当半年薪水的AI代币方能充分发挥生产力；并比喻不运用AI如同用纸笔设计芯片。
history.descriptionZh:
在NVIDIA GTC 2026大会的最后一天，公司首席执行官黄仁勋在参与《All-In》播客节目时，发表了一番引人深思的言论。当被问及NVIDIA是否为工程团队每年投入约20亿美元用于AI令牌（即调用AI模型进行计算所消耗的资源单位）时，黄仁勋给出了肯定意向，并借此阐述了他对AI工具如何重塑未来工作模式的激进看法。

黄仁勋的核心论点是：高价值的工程师必须大规模使用AI来放大其生产力。他提出了一个“思想实验”：假设一名软件工程师或AI研究员的年薪是50万美元，那么他在一年工作中消耗的AI令牌价值至少应达到25万美元（即年薪的一半）。如果其消耗仅有5000美元，他会感到“极度震惊”。他将不使用AI令牌的工程师比作拒绝使用CAD（计算机辅助设计）工具、坚持用纸笔设计芯片的工程师，认为这在当今时代是不可接受的。

在黄仁勋看来，当下的AI工具如同工业革命时期的机械，它们取代的不是体力，而是“心智苦役”。AI能够处理过去那些过于繁重、庞大或耗时的脑力任务，从而解放工程师，让他们专注于架构、创意和规范等更高层次的工作。他预测，未来的编程方式将发生根本性转变：“过去我们写代码，未来我们将撰写想法、架构和规格说明书。”他甚至设想，未来每位工程师都可能拥有上百个AI智能体（Agents）作为助手。

这种对AI令牌消耗的重视并非NVIDIA独有。据《商业内幕》报道，许多科技公司已开始将“保障性的AI推理算力访问”作为薪酬包的一部分提供给候选人。其背后的逻辑是，通过为员工提供充足的AI令牌资源，他们的生产效率可能被放大十倍或更多。

然而，在企业中广泛部署AI并非没有挑战和问题。有报告指出，超过半数的CEO尚未看到AI部署带来明显的效益，只有约12%的企业实现了收入增长和成本降低。此外，AI辅助的变革也带来了新的运营风险。例如，亚马逊AWS曾因“生成式AI辅助的变更”引发具有“高爆炸半径”（即影响范围广）的问题而召集工程师开会讨论。微软虽未直接归因于AI，但在其CEO透露高达30%的代码由AI生成后，公司也花费了数月时间来修复Windows 11的诸多问题以挽回声誉。

黄仁勋的言论清晰地勾勒出AI领头企业对未来工作流的愿景：将AI令牌的消耗视为一种必要的、生产性的投资，而不仅是成本。这标志着一个范式转变——衡量工程师价值的标准，可能将部分从其直接产出，转向其利用AI杠杆放大自身智能的能力。尽管前路伴随效率质疑与实施风险，但全力拥抱AI作为核心生产工具，已成为科技巨头推动下一波生产力革命的核心策略。
